top_signals.title: Russia overspends on Putin’s war in Ukraine by $28bn
top_signals.summary: Finance ministry asked cabinet in February to freeze expenditure in other areas as cost of conflict mounts
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Rise as Trump Says He’ll Make Call on Iran
top_signals.summary:
Hopes that a ceasefire deal could pave the way for an end to the Iran conflict drove stocks toward a historic streak of weekly gains, with the market also buoyed by the artificial-intelligence trade.
Brian Belski, CEO and CIO at Humilis Investment Strategies, discusses the S&P 500 bull case and his outlook for markets amid the AI boom. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Lula Rejects US Terrorism Designation for Brazil Drug Gangs
top_signals.summary: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva blasted a US decision to label two major Brazilian criminal groups as terrorist organizations, calling it a threat to the nation’s sovereignty that won’t help its fight against drug trafficking.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: What if remote working, not AI, is to blame for weak junior hiring?
top_signals.summary: New evidence suggests the rise of working from home has made entry-level hires a less attractive proposition
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Aave Labs secures dual UK licenses for regulated crypto payments infrastructure through local subsidiary
top_signals.summary: Aave Labs has obtained FCA cryptoasset registration in the U.K. for its Push subsidiaries, unlocking zero-fee fiat on-ramps.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Rio Tinto’s Aluminum Exports to US Rebound to Pretariff Levels
top_signals.summary: One of the world’s largest aluminum producers, Rio Tinto Group, says its shipments of the lightweight metal to the US have bounced back to levels from before President Donald Trump’s tariff offensive.
